uplink._note: Synthetic in-bounds payload. RH/temp halves are signed fixed-point (byte0=hundredths, byte1=integer): RH 45.25%, temp 72.5°F -> (72.5-32)*5/9 = 22.5°C. Counts are big-endian uint16. Raw decoded temp(72.5°F)/humidity(45.25%) confirmed against reference/upstream-codec.js decodeUplink.

uplink.description: Laird_Agg_TH: aggregated datalog, 2 readings share one RTC timestamp (0x02)
uplink._note: Synthetic in-bounds payload. RTC = 60s after 2015-01-01T00:00:00Z. Most recent reading (73.75°F/46.30% -> 23.19°C) at top level; the earlier reading (72.5°F/45.25% -> 22.5°C) goes to history with the shared timestamp. Raw temps(72.5,73.75°F)/humidities(45.25,46.30%) confirmed against reference/upstream-codec.js decodeUplink (readings array).

Synthetic in-bounds payload. Options byte 0x02 -> 'Sensor configuration error'. RTC = 120s after epoch. Raw temp(72.5°F)/humidity(45.25%) and timestamp confirmed against reference/upstream-codec.js decodeUplink.

uplink.description: SendBackLogMessages: multi-reading backlog datalog, per-reading timestamps (0x04)
uplink._note: Synthetic in-bounds payload. Each backlog reading carries its own 4-byte RTC. Most recent (t=20s, 73.75°F/46.30% -> 23.19°C) at top level; earlier (t=10s, 72.5°F/45.25% -> 22.5°C) in history. Raw readings + per-reading timestamps confirmed against reference/upstream-codec.js decodeUplink.

uplink.description: Laird_Simple_Config: simple config notification, no measurement (0x05)
uplink._note: Synthetic in-bounds payload. Config uplink carries no temp/RH, so only camelCase device-config extras are emitted. sensorReadPeriod is big-endian uint16 (0x0100 = 256). Field values confirmed against reference/upstream-codec.js decodeUplink (booleans normalized from upstream 'True'/'False').

uplink.description: Laird_Advanced_Config: advanced config with signed alarm limits (0x06)
uplink._note: Synthetic in-bounds payload. tempAlarmLimitLow byte 0xFF -> signed int8 -1. ledBle/ledLora are big-endian uint16. Field values confirmed against reference/upstream-codec.js decodeUplink (booleans normalized from upstream 'True'/'False').
Synthetic in-bounds payload. partNumber is big-endian uint32 (0x01020304 = 16909060). Field values confirmed against reference/upstream-codec.js decodeUplink.

uplink.description: Laird_Battery_Voltage: real battery voltage in volts (0x0A)
uplink._note: Synthetic in-bounds payload. The dedicated battery message carries a real voltage (signed fixed-point: byte0=hundredths 1, byte1=integer 3 -> 3.01 V), so it maps to the vocabulary `battery` key (volts) — unlike the coarse `batteryCapacity` bucket on TH messages. Confirmed against reference/upstream-codec.js decodeUplink (voltage 3.01).
Synthetic. bytes[0]=99 is not a defined uplink message type; reference/upstream-codec.js decodeUplink returns the same 'Invalid message type used!' error.
Synthetic. A 0x01 message must be exactly 11 bytes; reference/upstream-codec.js decodeUplink returns the same 'Invalid uplink message length!' error for a short payload.
